observations.archetype_gloss: Represents grief and the burden of loss that constricts and suffocates
observations.summary: Aviel embodies the Shadow archetype as he clings tightly to the remnants of what he loved, unable to let go, symbolizing the internal struggle with grief and loss.

evidence.quote: Some people throw everything away when they lose the thing they love most—burn it all, scatter the ashes, start fresh with empty hands. Others keep every thread, every cracked vessel, every scrap of parchment with nothing written on it, and pull it tighter and tighter until there is no room left to breathe. Aviel was one of those.

observations.archetype_gloss: The figure who undertakes a journey to undo the constriction caused by grief
observations.summary: Aviel's son is the Hero archetype, actively confronting and seeking to undo the suffocating grip of his father's grief, symbolizing a movement towards healing and liberation.

evidence.quote: Aviel was one of those. Which is relevant because it is exactly what his son was creeping through the dark to undo.
